codeReview.summary: The implementation fully covers all expected behaviors for parameter type validation. The ValidateParamType function correctly dispatches to type-specific validators for string, int, date, datetime, bool, and list types. ValidationError struct provides clear error messages with param name, expected type, and received value context. All 110+ test cases pass with 81.4% coverage. The code follows TDD practices with comprehensive test coverage including edge cases like leap years for date validation and multiple boolean representations for bool normalization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly used stretchr/testify for assertions as documented in AGENTS.md. No gaps found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/preset/params_test.go uses github.com/stretchr/testify/assert and github.com/stretchr/testify/require throughout

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed preset-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote tests first (TDD), ran verification tests, achieved 81.4% coverage exceeding the 80% minimum requirement documented in AGENTS.md.
No new services or commands required. All verification commands (go test, go build, go vet) are already documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Verification used: go test ./internal/preset/... -v -cover, go build ./..., go vet ./... - all in services.yaml

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture.md already documents the ValidationError struct and bool normalization approach. No new knowledge gaps discovered that need recording.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents Data Structures and Data Flows including parameter validation

summary: PASS - The preset-params-validation feature is fully implemented with comprehensive type validation for string, int, date, datetime, bool, and list types. The ValidationError struct provides clear error messages with all required context (param name, expected type, received value). Test coverage of 81.4% exceeds the 80% minimum requirement. Worker followed TDD practices and the preset-worker skill procedure correctly. No blocking issues found.
